title: At Dior, Jonathan Anderson is thinking long-term, not 'trendy'
subhed: One year after this arrival at Dior, the 41-year-old Northern Irish designer is still going at full throttle, releasing collections for Uniqlo and his own brand JW Anderson in addition to his 10 for the luxury label. His goal, he says, is to 'transform' the brand.

title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday

Lebanese government tracks war damage through scientific agency
subhed: Researchers at Lebanon's National Council for Scientific Research are documenting Israeli attacks in the country and the resulting damage. Their work aims to assess losses for potential legal action.

title: France prepares for another 'very intense and widespread' heat event, possibly over 40°C
subhed: The southern half of France is set to be hit first this weekend. Starting Tuesday, the heat is expected to worsen and move northward. Temperatures could reach or exceed 40°C, although some uncertainty remains.

title: Brazil make slow World Cup start with a 1-1 draw against Morocco
subhed: Vinicius Junior, who earned Brazil's one point with an equalizer, said his team paid the price for a sluggish start on Saturday. 'I don't think there's a lot to say now,' he said after the match held at MedLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. 'I think we really have to improve.'

title: NBA Finals: New York rediscovers passion for basketball and the Knicks
subhed: The New York Knicks have a chance to win the NBA championship for the first time since 1973 if they prevail on Saturday against Victor Wembanyama's San Antonio Spurs. Such a feat would mark a long-awaited reconciliation between New York and its storied franchise.

Josh O'Connor, star of Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day,' wants to 'cultivate his garden'
subhed: At 36, he is one of the most sought-after actors in both independent cinema and major Hollywood productions. In just a few years, the British artist, who is passionate about gardening, has put his delicate masculinity and subtle acting style to work for a wide range of characters, from a lovesick farmer to Prince Charles or a priest.

title: The Algerians who seek peaceful relations with France: 'For my children, I want us to reconcile and move on'
subhed: 'Algeria's unhealed war memories' (2/3). The trauma of independence remains deeply rooted in Algeria, affecting both pro-independence fighters and French conscripts. This fuels demands for recognition of colonial crimes and formal apologies, not just 'transactional diplomacy.'

title: David Hockney, giant of British painting, dies at the age of 88
subhed: Internationally renowned, notably for his painting 'A Bigger Splash' (1967) and his series of pool paintings, the British artist, who lived in Normandy from 2019 to 2023, produced work that straddled pop art and hyperrealism, never hesitating to use new technologies as sources of inspiration. He died on June 11.
